```{python}
        va = 'bottom')
texts = [plt.text(just_candy_props['likeness'].iloc[i],
                  just_candy_props['dislikeness'].iloc[i],
                  just_candy_props['label'].iloc[i],
                  fontsize = 6)
          for i in range(len(just_candy_props))]
adjust_text(texts,
  avoid_points = True)
ax.legend(title="Chocolate", loc = "center right")
ax.set_aspect('equal')
plt.show()
plt.close()

```

:::

<div class = "ethics">

When axes have the same limits, fixing the aspect ratio to 1:1 is the most ethical approach to avoid distorting data.

</div>

#### Simplifying labels and increasing text size

::: {.panel-tabset}

##### Description

```{r iter13aPot, fig.align = "center", echo = FALSE, out.width="100%"}
ottrpal::include_slide("https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1fu-KfdN2ldOXB49o9zdpurFbVvl1bOh8cfQ3vu0szk4/edit?slide=id.g3be338b6b90_0_99#slide=id.g3be338b6b90_0_99")
```

<!--
Maybe don't have the legend inside. Outside might be better/preferred since we have labels/text inside. At least discuss the options like direct labeling/etc.
-->

##### R code

```{r}
#| label: iter13aR
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-fold: show
#| code-summary: "R code for simplifying the legend"

to_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = JOY,
             y = DESPAIR,
             label = label,
             color = Chocolate,
             shape = Chocolate
            )
         ) +
  geom_point(size = 3,
             alpha = 0.6
            ) +
  xlim(0, 1) +
  ylim(1, 0) +
  theme_bw() +
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept=0.5), linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept=0.5), linetype = 'dashed') +
  annotate("richtext",
           label = "<span style='color: #CB2C2C;'>High</span> Joy<br>
                    <span style='color: #409AA6;'>Low</span> Despair",
           x = 0.955,
           y = 0.025,
           fill = NA,
           label.color = NA,
           label.padding = grid::unit(rep(0, 4), "pt")
          ) +
  annotate("richtext",
           label = "<span style='color: #CB2C2C;'>High</span> Joy<br>
                    <span style='color: #CB2C2C;'>High</span> Despair",
           x = 0.955,
           y = 0.975,
           fill = NA,
           label.color = NA,
           label.padding = grid::unit(rep(0, 4), "pt")
          ) +
  annotate("richtext",
           label = "<span style='color: #409AA6;'>Low</span> Joy<br>
                    <span style='color: #CB2C2C;'>High</span> Despair",
           x = 0.045,
           y = 0.975,
           fill = NA,
           label.color = NA,
           label.padding = grid::unit(rep(0, 4), "pt")
          ) +
  annotate("richtext",
           label = "<span style='color: #409AA6;'>Low</span> Joy<br>
                    <span style='color: #409AA6;'>Low</span> Despair",
           x = 0.045,
           y = 0.025,
           fill = NA,
           label.color = NA,
           label.padding = grid::unit(rep(0, 4), "pt")
          ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Yes" = "#543210",
                                "No" = "#00008B"),
                     labels = c("Not chocolate",                          #<40>
                                "Chocolate")) +                           #<40>
  geom_text_repel(show.legend = FALSE, max.overlaps = 16) +
  labs(x = "Fraction Joy",                                                #<41>
       y = "Fraction Despair",                                            #<41>
       title = "<b><span style='color: #543210 font-size: 30pt;'>Chocolate</span> is among the most liked candies in 2017</b>",  #<43>
       color = "") +                                                      #<42>
  theme(text = element_text(size = 22),                                   #<43>
        plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 0.5)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1)

```

40. Setting the display labels in the legend
41. Simplifying the axis labels
42. Turning off the legend title
43. Increasing the text size

##### Python code

```{python}
#| label: iter13aPython
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-fold: show
#| code-summary: "Python code for simplifying the legend"

mpl.rcParams["font.size"] = 14

plt.style.use('seaborn-v0_8-deep')
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.axhline(y=0.5, linestyle = 'dashed', color = 'black')
ax.axvline(x=0.5, linestyle = 'dashed', color = 'black')
scatter_nc = ax.scatter(just_candy_props['likeness'][mask_nc],just_candy_props['dislikeness'][mask_nc], c = just_candy_props['colors'][mask_nc], marker = "o", label = "Not chocolate", alpha = 0.6)
scatter_choc = ax.scatter(just_candy_props['likeness'][mask_choc],just_candy_props['dislikeness'][mask_choc], c = just_candy_props['colors'][mask_choc], marker = "^", label = "Chocolate", alpha = 0.6)
ax.set_xlim(0,1)
ax.set_ylim(1,0)
fig.canvas.draw()
ax_text(x = 0.88, y = 0.1,
        s='<High> Joy\n<Low> Despair',
        highlight_textprops=[{"color": '#CB2C2C'},
                             {"color": '#409AA6'}],
        fontsize = 10,
        ha = 'center',
        va = 'center',
        textalign = 'center',
        ax = ax)


ax_text(x = 0.88, y = 0.875,
        s = '<High> Joy\n<High> Despair',
        highlight_textprops=[{"color": '#CB2C2C'},
                             {"color": '#CB2C2C'}],
        fontsize = 10,
        ha = 'center',
        va = 'center',
        textalign = 'center',
        ax = ax)

ax_text(x = 0.125,  y = 0.875,
        s = '<Low> Joy\n<High> Despair',
        highlight_textprops=[{"color": '#409AA6'},
                             {"color": '#CB2C2C'}],
        fontsize = 10,
        ha = 'center',
        va = 'center',
        textalign = 'center',
        ax = ax)

ax_text(x = 0.125, y = 0.1,
        s = '<Low> Joy\n<Low> Despair',
        highlight_textprops=[{"color": '#409AA6'},
                             {"color": '#409AA6'}],
        fontsize = 10,
        ha = 'center',
        va = 'center',
        textalign = 'center',
        ax = ax)
ax.minorticks_on()
ax.grid(which='major', linestyle='-', linewidth='0.5', color='grey', alpha=0.7)
ax.grid(which='minor', linestyle=':', linewidth='0.3', color='grey', alpha=0.5)
ax.set_xlabel('Fraction Joy')
ax.set_ylabel('Fraction Despair')
fig_text(x = 0.5, y = 0.9,
        s = '<Chocolate> is among the most liked candies in 2017',
        highlight_textprops = [{"color": "#543210", "weight": "bold"}],
        textalign = 'center',
        ha = 'center',
        va = 'bottom',
        fontsize = 15)
texts = [plt.text(just_candy_props['likeness'].iloc[i],
                  just_candy_props['dislikeness'].iloc[i],
                  just_candy_props['label'].iloc[i],
                  fontsize = 6)
          for i in range(len(just_candy_props))]
adjust_text(texts,
  avoid_points = True)
ax.legend(bbox_to_anchor = (1.05, 0.5), title="", loc = "center left")
ax.set_aspect('equal')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
plt.close()

```

:::

::: {.panel-tabset}

##### Description

```{r iter13bPlot, fig.align = "center", echo = FALSE, out.width="100%"}
ottrpal::include_slide("https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1fu-KfdN2ldOXB49o9zdpurFbVvl1bOh8cfQ3vu0szk4/edit?slide=id.g3c26f54f9da_0_8#slide=id.g3c26f54f9da_0_8")
```

##### R code

```{r}
#| label: iter13bR
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| code-fold: show
#| code-summary: "R code for simplifying the legend"

to_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = JOY,
             y = DESPAIR,
             label = label,
             color = Chocolate,
             shape = Chocolate
            )
         ) +
  geom_point(size = 3,
             alpha = 0.6
            ) +
  xlim(0, 1) +
  ylim(1, 0) +
  theme_bw() +
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept=0.5), linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept=0.5), linetype = 'dashed') +
  annotate("richtext",
           label = "<span style='color: #CB2C2C;'>High</span> Joy<br>
                    <span style='color: #409AA6;'>Low</span> Despair",
           x = 0.955,
           y = 0.025,
           fill = NA,
           label.color = NA,
           label.padding = grid::unit(rep(0, 4), "pt")
          ) +
  annotate("richtext",
           label = "<span style='color: #CB2C2C;'>High</span> Joy<br>
                    <span style='color: #CB2C2C;'>High</span> Despair",
           x = 0.955,
           y = 0.975,
           fill = NA,
           label.color = NA,
           label.padding = grid::unit(rep(0, 4), "pt")
          ) +
  annotate("richtext",
           label = "<span style='color: #409AA6;'>Low</span> Joy<br>
                    <span style='color: #CB2C2C;'>High</span> Despair",
           x = 0.045,
           y = 0.975,
           fill = NA,
           label.color = NA,
           label.padding = grid::unit(rep(0, 4), "pt")
          ) +
  annotate("richtext",
           label = "<span style='color: #409AA6;'>Low</span> Joy<br>
                    <span style='color: #409AA6;'>Low</span> Despair",
           x = 0.045,
           y = 0.025,
           fill = NA,
           label.color = NA,
           label.padding = grid::unit(rep(0, 4), "pt")
          ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Yes" = "#543210",
                                "No" = "#00008B"),
                     labels = c("Not chocolate",
                                "Chocolate")) +
  scale_shape_discrete(labels = c("Not chocolate",                        #<44>
                                  "Chocolate")) +                         #<44>
  geom_text_repel(show.legend = FALSE, max.overlaps = 16) +
  labs(x = "Fraction Joy",
       y = "Fraction Despair",
       title = "<b><span style='color: #543210 font-size: 30pt;'>Chocolate</span> is among the most liked candies in 2017</b>",
       color = "",
       shape = "") +                                                      #<45>
  theme(text = element_text(size = 22),
        plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 0.5)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1)

```

44. Change the labels in the legend for shape too
45. Turn off the legend title for shape too

:::

#### The plot!

```{r finalPlot, fig.align = "center", echo = FALSE, out.width="100%"}
ottrpal::include_slide("https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1fu-KfdN2ldOXB49o9zdpurFbVvl1bOh8cfQ3vu0szk4/edit?slide=id.g3be338b6b90_0_146#slide=id.g3be338b6b90_0_146")
```

But we're not done yet....

#### The caption!

```{r plotCaption, fig.align = "center", echo = FALSE, out.width="100%"}
ottrpal::include_slide("https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1fu-KfdN2ldOXB49o9zdpurFbVvl1bOh8cfQ3vu0szk4/edit?slide=id.g3bb275f27df_0_20#slide=id.g3bb275f27df_0_20")
```

#### Going further

The dataset is an open dataset, meaning that it is available for you to explore and visualize on your own!

Perhaps you are interested in whether there's a difference in rankings depending on the sex of respondent or some other demographics. Do males prefer chocolate over non-chocolate candy? Does the age or location of the respondent impact the rankings?

Perhaps you are interested in how the proportion of indifference affects these rankings. Is there a design element that can be added to this plot to visualize that specifically?

Perhaps you'd like to try to plot this with a completely different plot type! Whatever your question -- go for it! Practice is one of the best ways to improve your data visualization skills.

## In Summary

Aim for understanding and readability over complexity. When you are working with your data and selecting your plot, think about what message you want to communicate.


## Checklist

This checklist contains reminders of considerations and steps you should take while building your expository data visualization in order to minimize its complexity, enhance its clarity and accessibility, and assess its accuracy.

## Keep Practicing!

Give some examples
